summary: Verified the package.json JSON syntax fix per scope (backend API testing skipped per main agent instructions). All 4 verification items pass: package.json is valid JSON, yarn install succeeds (up-to-date), both zod@3.24.4 and pdfjs-dist@3.9.179 are present in dependencies and installed in node_modules, and the frontend supervisor service is RUNNING with webpack compiling successfully (only non-blocking ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings).

verification_results.json_validity: PASS - python json.load succeeds; zod=3.24.4, pdfjs-dist=3.9.179
verification_results.yarn_install: PASS - 'success Already up-to-date. Done in 0.55s' (no JSON parse errors)
verification_results.supervisor_frontend_status: PASS - RUNNING (pid 1081); latest log shows 'Compiled with warnings' and 'webpack compiled with 1 warning' (ESLint only, non-blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: Out of scope but noted: Older entries in /var/log/supervisor/frontend.err.log show pre-fix yarn parse errors at position 2206 — these are historical, prior to the comma fix. Current/latest compile is clean (warnings only).

Out of scope: ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings exist in src/pages/student/Checkout.jsx (line 79) and src/pages/student/DigitalPdfViewer.jsx (lines 48, 66) — non-blocking, not related to this fix.
Out of scope: '@emergentbase/visual-edits not installed — visual editing disabled' message is informational only and not a blocking error.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Scope was strictly limited to verifying the single-comma JSON fix in /app/frontend/package.json between zod and pdfjs-dist entries. Backend was NOT tested (intentionally missing env vars like JWT_SECRET per main agent). Frontend UI flows were NOT tested. If future iterations need full E2E testing, the backend env config (JWT_SECRET, ADMIN_EMAIL, etc.) must be configured first.
rca of the issue: Original bug: missing comma between 'zod' and 'pdfjs-dist' lines in /app/frontend/package.json caused 'SyntaxError: Expected , or } after property value in JSON at position 2206', which broke yarn install and craco start. Fix: added the missing comma at line 66. Verification: json.load parses cleanly, yarn install succeeds, supervisor frontend runs and webpack compiles. Issue resolved.
